middleton-mayes.text: Mayes Middleton is a candidate for Attorney General of Texas, running with Republican Party (United States).
Mayes Middleton's recorded background: Mayes Middleton, state senator from the 11th district (2023–present).
No public data in our set on Mayes Middleton's campaign finance totals (this office is not required to file with the FEC, or no filing was found).

johnson-nathan.text: Nathan Johnson is a candidate for Attorney General of Texas, running with Democratic Party (United States).
Nathan Johnson's recorded background: Nathan Johnson, state senator from the 16th district (2019–present).
No public data in our set on Nathan Johnson's campaign finance totals (this office is not required to file with the FEC, or no filing was found).

oxford-tom.text: Tom Oxford is a candidate for Attorney General of Texas, running with Libertarian.
Tom Oxford's recorded background: Tom Oxford (Libertarian), attorney and perennial candidate
No public data in our set on Tom Oxford's campaign finance totals (this office is not required to file with the FEC, or no filing was found).

base.summary: Attorney General of Texas race: Mayes Middleton, Nathan Johnson, Tom Oxford. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and (for federal candidates only) FEC campaign finance totals. We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set; state executive offices generally do not file with the FEC, so campaign finance is not available for most of them here.

renter_young_worker.summary: For a renter young worker voter looking at the Attorney General of Texas race (Mayes Middleton, Nathan Johnson, Tom Oxford): our data set does not contain candidate-specific positions or voting records on housing costs, rent, or renters' protections for this race. renter_young_worker.caveats: We only show a connection to renter young worker concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.
